segments.text:
CYTOSKELETON
Functions
cell shape
organization of cell interior
whole cell movement
internal cell movement
Made of filaments and associated proteins

CYTOSKELETAL FILAMENTS
Microfilaments (7-9 nm diameter)
Intermediate filaments (10 nm)
Microtubules (25 nm)
Polymers of subunit proteins
Form both dynamic and stable structures

DYNAMIC STRUCTURES
Able to rearrange (polymerize and depolymerize)
Example – dividing cell
Green = microtubules
Red = microfilaments

STABLE STRUCTURES
Example – intestinal epithelial cells
Red = microfilaments
Green = microtubules
Blue = intermediate filaments

INTERMEDIATE FILAMENTS
Very strong rope-like structure
Basic subunit – tetramer
No polarity (so no motors)
Many different types

COMMON TYPES OF IFs
Keratins – epithelial cells
Lamins – nuclear lamina
Neurofilaments – axons
